shellSubtitle: Core access and leaderboard guard check
debugSubtitle: Operate and verify guard, cache, and rate-limit behavior

profile.title: Physical profile
profile.subtitle: Used as the reference for body-weight and age-based scoring. Stored on this device (Core, no account required).
profile.completeBadge: Baseline complete — ready for assessment inputs that use height, weight, age, and gender.
profile.incompleteHint: Add your body data so later assessments can convert real metrics to scores.

errors.invalid-gender: Gender must be male or female.
errors.invalid-height: Height must be between 120 and 230 cm.
errors.invalid-weight: Weight must be between 35 and 250 kg.

home.overallFormula: Sum of six core scores ÷ 6 (arm circumference excluded).

ladder.title: Live ladder
ladder.body: You have Core + Pro access. Rankings load through the leaderboard service.
ladder.bodyRemote: Connected to Firestore — summary rows only, with client cache + best-score writes.
ladder.bodyLocalMock: Firebase env not set — showing in-memory mock data for development.
ladder.noNetworkNote: Non-Pro accounts never hit Firestore from this route.
ladder.cacheHit: Serving from local session cache (TTL applies).
ladder.loadError: Could not load rankings. Check Firebase rules and indexes.
ladder.empty: No pilots on this shard yet — be the first upload.

community.title: Community uplink
community.subtitle: Squads, challenges, and shared missions will dock here — the route is live, feature modules ship next.
community.card1Body: Future: crew channels, shoutouts, and spotlight runs without touching your local training data.
community.card2Body: Tie-ins to ladders stay Pro-gated — Community itself never phones home until you explicitly share.

tools.title: Tools & cloud sync
tools.subtitle: Assist systems for backup, locale, and future calculators — minimal, fast, no noise.
tools.consoleBody: HUD calibration, latency probes, and dev panels will anchor in this slot. Local-first stays default.
tools.languageHint: Flip between English and Traditional Chinese for field tests.
tools.syncBody: Pro subscribers can snapshot scores + local history to Firestore (single document, JSON payload).
tools.syncProOnly: Cloud sync requires Pro — open Join Arena if you need the uplink.

sync.backupOk: Backup written to your Pro cloud slot.
sync.backupFail: Backup unavailable (check Pro + Firebase + anonymous auth).
sync.restoreOk: Local scores and history replaced from cloud snapshot.
sync.restoreFail: Restore failed — verify Pro, Firebase config, and network.

history.title: Assessment history
history.subtitle: Snapshots saved from Assessment (device-only, Core — no subscription required).
history.empty: No snapshots yet. Save one from the Assessment tab.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.subtitle: Enter raw scores per axis (0–200; typical manual entry stays 0–100; body-fat may exceed 100 after FFMI). Totals update on Home (linear average ÷ 6).
assessment.localFirstNote: Core-only: scores stay on device — no Firebase on this screen.
assessment.homeRadarHint: Radar and overall average are shown on Home.

ffmi.title: FFMI (body-fat axis)
ffmi.body: Compute FFMI from Home profile + body fat %. Radar accepts the score only while adjusted FFMI is at or below the world-record anchor for your sex.

cardio.title: Cardio (Cooper / 5 km)
cardio.body: 12-minute Cooper test with age- and sex-based standards, or 5 km time (20 min → 100+; 45 min → 0). Raw inputs are stored locally; the radar prefers Cooper when a valid distance is saved.

muscle.title: Muscle mass (SMM)
muscle.body: Skeletal muscle mass (kg) versus your saved weight, age, and sex — same norm tables as the reference app; final score is the simple average of the SMM and SM% branch scores. Saved SMM updates the muscle radar axis locally.

explosive.title: Explosive power (jump / sprint)
explosive.body: Vertical jump (cm), standing long jump (cm), and sprint time (seconds) with age- and sex-based tables from the reference app — enter at least one metric; the score is the mean of the branch raw scores (limit-break above 100 when norms allow). Raw inputs stay on device; Home radar prefers this axis when valid saves exist.

strength.title: Five-lift strength (DOTS-based)
strength.body: Bench press, squat, deadlift, lat pulldown, and standing barbell shoulder press — each lift has its own Calculate button and score; use the combined section to preview the radar axis (mean of all completed rows) before saving. Grip strength is a separate assessment. Raw inputs stay on device.

strength.title: Strength assessment
strength.subtitle: Five lifts with age- and sex-adjusted scoring (DOTS-style anchors). Use Calculate on each row for that lift’s score; use Calculate combined for the radar-axis mean of every row that has weight and reps (1–10).
strength.repsAccuracyNudge: Fewer reps usually make the estimated 1RM more reliable. If you have a heavier, lower-rep set taken near failure, prefer entering that weight and rep count.
strength.fieldsHint: Uses gender, age, and body weight from your Home physical profile. Leave both fields empty for a lift you are skipping. Reps above 10 are not accepted (submaximal estimate quality). Each lift also has a model weight ceiling — values above it are clamped for scoring and storage, with an on-screen notice.
strength.combinedHint: Averages every row that has both weight and reps. Rows with one field empty are ignored here (but must not be “half filled” — clear both or fill both).
strength.capWeightNotice: {{lift}}: you entered {{input}} kg, which exceeds this movement’s model ceiling ({{max}} kg). Scoring and saved weight use {{max}} kg — aligned with elite / all-time raw or strongman reference context for this lift (not a meet certification).
strength.branchLine: {{weight}} kg × {{reps}} reps · est. 1RM {{oneRm}} kg · lift score {{score}}
strength.radarClampNote: If the mean exceeds the per-axis cap (0–200), the stored radar value is clamped.
strength.submitDone: Saved — Home radar and merged scores use this strength axis.
strength.profileIncompleteHint: Strength scoring needs a complete Home profile (sex, age, height, weight).

howToInfo.title: Scoring notes
howToInfo.intro: Each completed lift is converted to an estimated 1RM (Brzycki), then to a DOTS-style score adjusted for age (McCulloch) and normalized against built-in anchor totals for that lift — matching the reference fitness stack.
howToInfo.reps: Use 1–10 reps so the 1RM estimate stays in a reliable range; enter the weight you actually lifted for that set.
howToInfo.repsAccuracy: Within that range, lower rep counts are generally more trustworthy for Brzycki extrapolation; very high-rep sets can yield a more optimistic estimated 1RM, which may not match a “heavier is always stronger” gut check.
howToInfo.tip: Warm up and use consistent depth / range of motion when comparing over time. This screen is not a competition verification tool.

errors.missing-profile: Add a complete physical profile on Home before calculating strength.
errors.no-inputs: Enter at least one lift with both weight and reps.
errors.pair-incomplete: For each lift, enter both weight and reps, or leave both fields empty.
errors.invalid-weight: Enter a positive weight (kg) for each completed lift.
errors.invalid-reps: Enter a valid rep count for each completed lift.
errors.reps-out-of-range: Reps must be between 1 and 10 for each completed lift.

singleErrors.missing-profile: Add a complete physical profile on Home first.
singleErrors.empty-row: Enter weight and reps for this lift, then calculate.
singleErrors.pair-incomplete: Enter both weight and reps for this lift, or clear both fields.
singleErrors.invalid-weight: Enter a positive weight (kg) for this lift.
singleErrors.invalid-reps: Enter a valid rep count for this lift.
singleErrors.reps-out-of-range: Reps must be between 1 and 10 for this lift.

cardio.title: Cardio assessment
cardio.subtitle: Cooper 12-minute run (meters) or 5 km time. Cooper needs a complete Home profile; 5 km time only needs your finish time.
cardio.cooperHint: Run on a track or measure with GPS. Model ceiling aligned with elite 5,000 m standards: {{maleCap}} m (male), {{femaleCap}} m (female); scores cap above that.
cardio.cooperWorldRecordCapHint: That distance exceeds our model ceiling for elite 5,000 m–class performance ({{capMeters}} m for your profile). Scoring uses the capped distance.

cooperInfo.title: Cooper 12-minute run test
cooperInfo.p1: The 12-minute run field test is widely credited to Dr. Kenneth H. Cooper, former U.S. Air Force colonel, as a practical aerobic screening tool. Gold-standard VO₂ max is usually measured in a lab under maximal effort, which is hard to scale; the Cooper protocol uses maximal distance covered in 12 minutes as a field estimate of cardiorespiratory fitness / VO₂ max—and it has roots in military fitness screening. (Educational/fitness context only—not a medical diagnosis.)
cooperInfo.p2: How to perform: use a track or another safe, flat route; record total meters via lap counting or a sports watch / GPS; do a 10–15 minute dynamic warm-up beforehand to reduce injury risk.
cooperInfo.p3: Your app score maps sex and age to a norm distance table: 60-point and 100-point anchor distances differ by age band, so the same distance can yield very different scores. We linearly interpolate between the 60- and 100-point anchors and allow scores above 100 beyond the top anchor. This app provides a Cooper age band from 13+.
cooperInfo.p4: Norm distances follow the same lineage as commonly published Cooper test charts (including references to chart compendia such as the Cooper Test Chart) and secondary citations of chart work associated with Carl Magnus Swahn. Institutions may use slightly different bands; this app interpolates using the same anchor table as our fitness reference implementation.

cardio.run5kmHint: Faster than 20:00 scores above 100; 45:00 or slower is 0. Times are stored on device only.
cardio.submitDone: Saved — Home radar and history can use this score.
cardio.profileIncompleteHint: Cooper needs age and sex from your Home profile. You can still use the 5 km tab with time only.

errors.missing-profile: Add a complete physical profile on Home to use Cooper.
errors.missing-profile-cooper: Set age and sex on Home before running Cooper.
errors.invalid-cooper-distance: Enter a positive distance in meters.
errors.cooper-no-standard: No Cooper table for this age range — check the age on your profile (13+).
errors.invalid-5km-time: Enter a valid 5 km time (minutes and/or seconds).

explosive.title: Explosive power assessment
explosive.subtitle: Vertical jump, standing long jump, and sprint — same norm tables as the reference app. Enter at least one field; scoring averages the raw scores from each completed metric.
explosive.fieldsHint: Uses age and sex from your Home physical profile. Norm tables cover ages 12–80 (this app’s profile starts at 13; ages above 80 are out of table range). Sprint uses the same decreasing-time curve as the reference implementation. For product sanity, each field also has an elite-record–class model ceiling or floor (see notices after Calculate when an input is adjusted).
explosive.capVerticalJump: Vertical jump was capped at {{max}} cm — above typical NBA/NFL elite combine context. Scores and saved values use the capped measurement.
explosive.capStandingLongJump: Standing long jump was capped at {{max}} cm — aligned with documented elite combine / track marks (e.g. Byron Jones class). Scores and saved values use the capped measurement.
explosive.capSprint100m: 100 m time was floored to {{min}} s — faster inputs are treated as elite world-record class for the model. Scores and saved values use the floored time.
explosive.averageRawLabel: Average of entered tests (mean of branch scores)
explosive.radarClampNote: Radar axis uses your profile score cap (0–200) when the formula average is outside that range.
explosive.submitDone: Saved — Home radar and merged scores use this explosive axis.
explosive.profileIncompleteHint: Explosive scoring needs a complete Home profile (sex, age, height, weight).

howToInfo.title: Test instructions
howToInfo.verticalJump: Measure vertical jump height (cm): standing reach to your highest touch point, then jump and touch as high as you can — the difference is your vertical jump. It mainly reflects lower-body explosiveness.
howToInfo.standingLongJump: Standing long jump (cm): stand with toes on a take-off line, jump without a run-up, and measure from the line (toes) to the nearest landing mark at the heels. It reflects leg power and coordination.
howToInfo.sprint: 100 m sprint (seconds): start from rest, sprint 100 m at full effort, and record finish time — lower seconds yield a higher score in this app’s model.
howToInfo.tip: Warm up thoroughly before testing. Prefer a standard track or measured course and consistent protocol so you can compare results over time.

standardsInfo.title: Norm tables & scoring context
standardsInfo.disclaimer: Scores map your raw measurements to age- and sex-based reference curves in this app — for fitness self-tracking only, not a medical diagnosis.
standardsInfo.anchorsIntro: For your current profile, approximate raw anchors at 0 / 50 / 100 points are:
standardsInfo.anchorSprintLine: 100 m sprint (s) — 0 / 50 / 100 pts ≈ {{v0}} / {{v50}} / {{v100}} (faster time = higher score)
standardsInfo.noAnchorsHint: No norm row applies to the age on your Home profile (tables cover about 12–80). Adjust age or check the profile.
standardsInfo.profileIncompleteForAnchors: Complete your Home physical profile (including age and sex) to see anchor values for your band.
standardsInfo.sourceBody: Public fitness norms and field-test literature — for example national youth fitness portals, ACSM guidance, World Athletics / championship sprint context, and school athletics standards — inform the same anchor structure as our earlier reference implementation.
standardsInfo.basisVjump: Vertical jump: ACSM-style field norms and youth performance bands.
standardsInfo.basisSlj: Standing long jump: school-age norms and decline curves similar to ACSM summaries.
standardsInfo.basisSprint: 100 m: sprint time norms aligned with championship-tier tables used in the reference scoring model.
standardsInfo.remark: Full-span data for every single age is not always published; where needed, the reference tables use interpolated bands (for example decade-based decline assumptions and sex-separated anchors). Your displayed anchors are the exact row used for scoring in this app.

errors.missing-profile: Add a complete physical profile on Home first.
errors.age-out-of-range: No explosive-power norm table for this age (tables run through 80). Check the age on your profile.
errors.power-no-standard: Could not resolve norm rows — verify age and sex on Home.
errors.no-inputs: Enter at least one metric (vertical jump, standing long jump, or sprint time).
errors.invalid-vertical-jump: Vertical jump must be empty or a positive number (cm).
errors.invalid-standing-long-jump: Standing long jump must be empty or a positive number (cm).
errors.invalid-sprint: Sprint time must be empty or a positive number (seconds).

muscle.title: Muscle mass assessment
muscle.subtitle: Enter skeletal muscle mass (SMM, kg) from a scale or scan. Scoring uses the same age- and sex-based norm tables as the reference implementation.
muscle.smmHint: Uses weight, age, and sex from your Home physical profile. Norm tables support ages roughly 10–80; this app’s profile starts at 13, and ages above 80 are out of table range.
muscle.smmCeilingLine: Accepted SMM for scoring: up to {{max}} kg for your sex (male cap ~ Ronnie Coleman–class public numbers; female cap ~ top-tier pro stage).

standardsInfo.title: Muscle score — what we measure
standardsInfo.p1: Skeletal muscle mass (SMM) is the kilograms of muscle typically reported by body-composition devices (for example BIA scales or professional scans). Enter the value your device shows; we do not estimate SMM from height or photos here.
standardsInfo.p2: Skeletal muscle percentage (SM%) is SMM ÷ body weight × 100, using the weight saved on Home. Absolute SMM and SM% answer different questions: a high SMM with a high body weight can still yield a moderate SM%, and vice versa. Using both reduces one-sided readings.
standardsInfo.p3: Each branch is scored against age- and sex-specific norm tables (decile anchors from 0 to 100 points). Between anchors we linearly interpolate; beyond the top anchor the model can extend the branch score above 100 on a damped curve — so elite readings are not artificially flattened. Norm bands in this app run roughly ages 10–80; your Home profile must be at least 13 and at most 80 for a table row to apply.
standardsInfo.p4: Your radar muscle axis uses the simple arithmetic mean of the SMM branch score and the SM% branch score (equal weight). Preview and “Save to radar” use the same calculation so what you see is what gets stored.
standardsInfo.p5: For product sanity we also apply a maximum SMM input by sex (shown above). Values above that are treated as outside the scoring model — not a medical judgment. If your device reads higher, double-check calibration, posture, or pick a consistent measurement protocol.

muscle.submitDone: Saved — Home radar and merged scores use this muscle axis.
muscle.profileIncompleteHint: Muscle scoring needs a complete Home profile (sex, age, height, weight).

errors.missing-profile: Add a complete physical profile on Home first.
errors.invalid-smm: Enter a positive SMM in kilograms.
errors.age-out-of-range: No muscle norm table for this age (supported through 80). Check the age on your profile.
errors.smm-exceeds-ceiling: SMM is above the model ceiling ({{max}} kg). We cap male inputs around Ronnie Coleman–class public composition levels and female inputs around elite pro stage ranges. Enter {{max}} kg or lower to calculate and save — double-check your device reading if this looks wrong.
errors.standards-not-found: Could not resolve norm rows — verify age and sex on Home.

ffmi.title: FFMI assessment
ffmi.subtitle: Fat-free mass index from your saved physical profile plus body-fat %. If adjusted FFMI exceeds the documented world-record anchor for your sex, saving to the radar is blocked until you adjust inputs — future versions may add an overclock visualization.
ffmi.bodyFatHint: Reasonable inputs are about 3–60%. Uses height, weight, and gender from Home.
ffmi.worldRecordLockIntro: Your adjusted FFMI is above the documented elite-record anchor for your sex. The radar cannot plot this run yet — change body fat or profile data until you are at or below the limit. A dedicated overclock mode for the chart is planned.
ffmi.submitRadar: Write score to radar (body fat axis)
ffmi.submitDone: Stored locally — radar on Home updates.

errors.missing-profile: Physical profile incomplete — add gender, age, height, and weight on Home first.
errors.invalid-body-fat: Body fat must be between 3 and 60%.
errors.world-record-lock: Adjusted FFMI is above the world-record anchor — adjust body fat or profile before writing to the radar.

info.whatIsIntro: FFMI (Fat-Free Mass Index) reflects lean mass versus height and body fat — often more informative than BMI alone for training context. Higher values generally mean more fat-free mass among people with similar height. Results can skew when:

caveats.tall: Height is far above average (around ≥190 cm). This app applies a tall correction above 1.8 m.
caveats.highFat: Body fat percentage is unusually high relative to typical reporting ranges.
caveats.heavy: Body weight is unusually high for height, which affects estimated fat-free mass.

table.sectionHint: Bands are educational labels only — not a medical or PED diagnosis.
